description_long: This is a long, single-edged sword with a gently curved blade, reminiscent of a traditional Japanese katana. The blade displays a gradient of colors, transitioning from blue and green near the hilt to purple and red toward the tip, giving it a striking, decorative appearance. The handle is wrapped in a crisscross pattern, likely simulating traditional cord wrapping, and matches the color gradient of the blade. A circular guard (tsuba) separates the blade from the handle, providing both functional protection and a visual break in the design. The sword appears to be constructed primarily from metal, with the handle possibly wrapped in fabric or synthetic material for grip. There are no visible engravings or additional ornamentation beyond the color pattern.
description: A colorful, curved single-edged sword with a wrapped handle and circular guard, featuring a gradient from blue to red. The design is sleek and decorative, resembling a katana.
description_view_0: The sword is seen at an angle, highlighting the full length of the blade and the color gradient from blue at the handle to red at the tip.
description_view_1: A direct edge-on view, showing the thinness of the blade and the circular shape of the guard.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the sword, again displaying the color gradient and the curvature of the blade.
description_view_3: A direct side view, emphasizing the sword's length, curvature, and the evenness of the color transition along the blade and handle.
attribute_handle_design: straight, wrapped in a crisscross pattern, synthetic or fabric grip
